name: Bonaire (Caribbean Netherlands)

colors.symbolism: the compass rose, representing the four cardinal directions
love of the people for the island, on the six-pointed star

description: Divided diagonally from upper hoist to lower fly: a yellow triangle in the upper hoist and a blue triangle in the lower fly, separated by a narrow white diagonal band. A black-bordered white compass rose with a red six-pointed star at its centre is placed in the upper-hoist near the centre.
history: Adopted on 11 December 1981. Designed by Whitney Newton Booi, the flag became official as Bonaire moved toward greater autonomy within the Netherlands Antilles; it remained in use after Bonaire became a special municipality of the Netherlands on 10 October 2010.

features.symbolism: A diagonal yellow and blue field divided by a narrow white stripe, with the upper-hoist a yellow triangle and the lower-fly a blue triangle.

features.role: compass rose with arrows pointing to the four cardinal directions
features.symbolism: the four cardinal directions; Bonaire's people, who came from all parts of the world

features.role: six-pointed star within the compass rose
features.symbolism: the six original villages of Bonaire (Antriol, Nikiboko, Noord Salinja, Playa, Rincon, and Tera Cora)
